_meta.description: Plug load density based on reference standard and building type. Unlike equipment density (d_67), this is NOT a 3D lookup - it's based on energy standard requirements and occupancy patterns.

inputs.name: building.referenceStandard
inputs.description: Reference standard (d_13) - e.g., 'OBC SB10', 'PH Classic', 'NECB T1'

invariants.description: Plug load density is always positive

behavior.condition: standard contains 'PH Classic' OR 'PH Plus' OR 'PH Premium'
behavior.note: Passive House certified standards have strictest plug load requirements

behavior.condition: standard contains 'PHIUS' OR 'EnerPHit' OR 'PH Low Energy'
behavior.note: Other Passive House standards have moderate plug load requirements

behavior.condition: buildingType contains 'C-Residential' OR 'C - Residential' OR 'B1-Detention' OR 'B1 - Detention' OR 'B2-Care' OR 'B2 - Care' OR 'B3-Detention' OR 'B3 - Detention'
behavior.note: Residential and care occupancies have lower plug loads than commercial. Both hyphenated (B1-Detention) and spaced (B1 - Detention) forms are matched.

rationale.whyNotLookupTable: Plug loads depend on usage patterns and energy standards, not equipment efficiency. PH standards mandate lower plug loads as part of certification. Residential buildings naturally have lower plug loads than offices. A 3D lookup (buildingType × efficiency × elevators) doesn't make sense because plug loads aren't affected by 'Efficient vs Regular' equipment specs.
rationale.contrastWithEquipment: Equipment density (d_67) uses a 3D lookup because actual equipment installed varies by efficiency spec and elevator presence. Plug loads are about what users plug in, not building equipment.
